reasoning: VIX at 18.7 is in MID regime but rising sharply (+7.84%), a significant bearish signal. Overnight gap of -7.69 down indicates material bearish overnight sentiment. BEAR ribbon structure (269¢ spread) reinforces bearish directional bias. Two medium-severity events today (Empire State 08:30, Industrial Production 09:15) are premarket/early-RTH — they set opening tone but don't hard-block RTH trading. However, conflicting rotation_signal='risk_on' and some sector strength (XLK +1.21%, XLE +0.73%) create moderate uncertainty. Overall macro backdrop is risk-off building, but not extreme (VIX not >22). Bearish bias favored for setups targeting downside; bullish entries need extra confirmation.
key_observations: VIX rising +7.84% (18.7, was ~17.3 yesterday implied) — elevated fear building despite MID regime. Historically this pattern precedes selloff acceleration.
Overnight gap -7.69 down is material (> 1% move on $748 close). Signals significant overnight risk-off sentiment.
BEAR ribbon stack with 269-cent spread indicates structural bearish alignment. Fast EMA 740.67 vs Pivot 741.27 vs Slow 743.36 — bears below moving averages.
Rotation signal 'risk_on' conflicts with physical VIX + gap data. Possible lagging data or inter-market divergence (XLK strength vs broad risk-off).

Two medium-severity macro events are premarket/early-RTH (08:30, 09:15). Industrial Production at 09:15 creates whipsaw window 09:10-09:30 (right at open), then likely fades by 09:45 per historical pattern.
